title: Eva Unit 03 Set Up
wikitext:
[]: Eva Unit 03--Set Up?             Quote Post by fadingreminder » Sun Mar 11, 2007 7:07 pm I've always been curious about this... From all the information we can gather, an Evangelion needs a soul and a pilot to properly function. The three main Evas seem to contain souls, but as for Unit 03, it was never really explained. Kaworu mentioned that in being from Adam himself, that if the Evangelions did not have a soul, he could freely enter them at will. If Seele had been harboring Kaworu since Second Impact, surely they would know of this too. That being said, is it possible that they realized if they sent an Eva without a soul to Gendo, that it would surely be entered by another Angel? This is something I've always been curious about. Seele seems to have always known about Gendo's possible treachery and what he may plan on unleashing with Unit 01. Do you think Seele saw this as a possibility to further their own plans? The rest of the Eva series would not have been able to be unleashed without a dummy plug. Seele seems to have spies everywhere and constantly gather information. They themselves may have even chosen Toji as a pilot. Say they had, they would have to know about him and they would have to know about his friendship with Shinji. Since they know this much about Toji, an outsider at this time, then imagine the information they have on Shinji. Perhaps they knew that he would be unable to fight and the dummy plug would have to be used in a field test. I haven't really heard anything to give any backdrop to what happened with Unit-03 or whether it had a soul or really anything substancial for that matter. This is just something I've noticed and always thought for what end? Who would benefit? With as much as the string pullers seem to know, wouldn't they know it couldn't work? Well, maybe they did... Maybe I have too many theories about Evangelion and all the conspiracies therein.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Sun Mar 11, 2007 8:30 pm By a process of inference, we can assume with some certainty that Unit-03 contained the soul of Touji's mother. Though we do not actually explicitly hear that his mother is dead, we do have indirect evidence . Your point about the Eva's having no souls making them easier to "merge" with is important. However, there is evidence in episode #13 that even the Eva's with sould could be taken over in this manner. Gendou orders the launch of the Eva's before the pilots can even return, saying that if the Eva's are infected it is all over. So it appears that Touji's mother can still be in the Eva, even though it was taken over. There's also evidence of Evas being taken over in episode #23' and even again in episode #18 when Bardiel appears to try and infect Unit-00. Here's another thread discussing Unit-03 in some detail.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Sun Mar 11, 2007 8:36 pm Kaworu meant if they didn't have souls he could enter their core and effectively turn an Eva into Adam-MkII. Body possesion isn't quite the same thing.

[]:             Quote Post by Fearthebait » Sun Mar 11, 2007 8:42 pm Anonymous_Evafan wrote: Kaworu meant if they didn't have souls or if the soul refused to take dominance he could enter their core and effectively turn an Eva into Adam-MkII. Body possesion isn't quite the same thing.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Sun Mar 11, 2007 8:46 pm Meh, we don't know what would have happened if he tried. Might have been something really funny.

[]:             Quote Post by Fearthebait » Sun Mar 11, 2007 9:08 pm something like: Its time to du du du du dud dud dud du DUEL!

[]:             Quote Post by fadingreminder » Mon Mar 12, 2007 1:23 pm Okay, ObsessiveMathsFreak, so those are some good points I never really noticed. I also recognize that Evas can be and have been contaminated by Angels even though they have souls. As for a counter-point, I don't think Toji's father and grandfather work at NERV. It wasn't until Toji became a Pilot that NERV decided to foot his sister's bill. If his family were already NERV workers, then at that point, wouldn't NERV had already been paying the medical bills? Also, after the incident with Asuka's mother, they put her into the Eva program immediately. Wouldn't they have done the same with Toji if his mother were also working so closely with Project E? Once again though, I recognize that there are exceptions. Shinji wasn't thrust into the position of Evangelion pilot right after his mother's incident, but at the same time, Project E had not yet advanced enough. It seems to me that they chose Asuka maybe 2 years or so after Shinji's mother died. Shinji didn't seem to be old enough to speak or really even remember much about what really happened, while Asuka was old enough to speak and retain those memories. In those few years, the Eva Project could have come far enough for them to realize the secrets of the Evangelions and know they needed the child. As for the Kaworu reference, what I mean by that is Kaworu could only control Unit 02 externally. He could not quite enter the core or become one with the Eva. Other Angels have shown capabilities of infecting Evas, but even then, I wouldn't necessarily say they could enter the core or merge. The Angels that have tried to infect or make direct contact with Eva or the pilots only seem to be able to inflict pain or unravel the mind. They don't seem to have the ability to actually control an Eva. The one exception is Iruel. The ability of which it could reprogram the Magi may lend to the theory that it could hijack an Eva. Not enough is really known there though; it may only be able to affect the Evangelions' mechanics, which would also be a deadly threat. Gendo launching the Evas to keep them away from the Angel was a smart move due to the traits the Angel was displaying. Even it was only capable of mechanical alteration or computer reprogramming, it would still be possible for the Angel to erase all the pilot's data or wipe out the battery or take care of the technical aspect of the Eva and render it useless. The time it might take to reprogram or readjust all the Eva data may be too much as if another Angel attacks before they're reset, then NERV is left defenseless. Getting back on topic though, I don't really buy into Toji's mother being in Unit-03. There just isn't enough evidence to convince me and there's too much pointing against the possibility of his family's involvement with NERV to convince me. If Kensuke goes crazy over Shinji for his involvement, he'd never let up on Toji either.

[]: Re: Eva Unit 03--Set Up?             Quote Post by Good_Noodle » Mon Mar 12, 2007 2:56 pm fadingreminder wrote: They themselves may have even chosen Toji as a pilot.

[]:             Quote Post by fadingreminder » Tue Mar 13, 2007 3:01 pm Going back to the Kaworu point though, perhaps they didn't consider, or even want, Unit-03 to be inhabited by another Angel. Maybe Unit-03 could have been soulless for the possibility of Kaworu inhabiting it later. Seele is probably aware of Unit-01 and the soul it holds or Gendo's plans for Unit-01. After every other Angel has been destroyed, if Unit-03 had still been around, then that would present the possibility of Kaworu creating the Adam-MkII (cool term). Once again, it's nothing I can really prove or disprove, just a theory I've had. Not only that, but I think it's fun to throw ideas and conspiracy theories like this around. I'm a firm believer that there is not one single episode of Evangelion that is "filler". Even the Jet Alone episode, which I didn't really consider very important on first viewing, I came to accept as pivotal. With that, I believe Seele or NERV themselves had to have a mole inside to sabotage the whole thing. That could lead to two great happenings for NERV. First, it makes it look like an Eva just saved a population from a nuclear blast and saved the day, thus making NERV look capable and probably getting favor with the public and government officials. Also, if JA was successful, then there surely would've been others since it would be cheaper and easy to mass produce than an Eva and could result in having funding cut for the project or even worse, having an army of robots ready to dispose of the Evas for when the Instrumentality Project rolls out. Back to the matter at hand, I thought Marduk was a front for Seele and one of their factions rather than one of NERVs? If that's the case, then that's a really good point.

[]:             Quote Post by fadingreminder » Tue Mar 13, 2007 5:32 pm I knew that Marduk was a puppet syndicate, I just didn't realize it was controlled by NERV themselves, I could've sworn it was a Seele project... oh well. As for the Adam-MkII, I only heard it coined here as Kaworu + Eva core = Adam-MkII.

[]:             Quote Post by Good_Noodle » Tue Mar 13, 2007 5:32 pm Yeah, the Markduk Institute is baloney, and NERV is behind it. It is clearly stated in one of the episodes, not sure which one exactly though and too lazy to scan through them. I completely agree with you on the JetAlone episode. It is from that episode where viewers begin to see the tip of the iceberg of NERV's true nature. There really aren't any blatant fillers in NGE (and why should it? it's a 26-episode OVA); even the early monster-a-week angel episodes served to develop character or uncover plot, like the Isfarel episode, for example. And before it drives me insane: What is Adam-MkII?

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Tue Mar 13, 2007 7:03 pm fadingreminder wrote: As for a counter-point, I don't think Toji's father and grandfather work at NERV. It wasn't until Toji became a Pilot that NERV decided to foot his sister's bill. If his family were already NERV workers, then at that point, wouldn't NERV had already been paying the medical bills? Also, after the incident with Asuka's mother, they put her into the Eva program immediately. Wouldn't they have done the same with Toji if his mother were also working so closely with Project E?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Mar 13, 2007 10:04 pm More time paradoxes... Good Noodle asks what "Adam MkII" is AFTER fadingreminder provides the answer. ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: It is certain that Nerv sabotaged JA's launch, with Kaji as a tenuous possibility for the person that uploaded the program, presumably written by computer genius Ritsuko, into the system.

[]:             Quote Post by Tokpile Quohog » Tue Mar 13, 2007 10:12 pm It's more convenient to just make Kaji do all the secret sabotage/spy stuff. That way they don't have to mention any more characters or draw any more.

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Tue Mar 13, 2007 11:04 pm Reichu wrote: ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: It is certain that Nerv sabotaged JA's launch, with Kaji as a tenuous possibility for the person that uploaded the program, presumably written by computer genius Ritsuko, into the system.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Mar 13, 2007 11:32 pm Off in Deutschland indeed he is. Unlike Miyamura, though, who does random bit parts from #01 (I believe) on up, Yamadera is JUST "Mysterious Guy On Phone" in #07, and then Kaji shows up the next episode. I wouldn't mind seeing something at least at least vaguely Official comment on the matter...

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Wed Mar 14, 2007 3:14 am Well, assuming Kaji is the mysterious international man of mystery type, it's not unreasonable to suggest he could sabotage JA in Japan and be back in Germany for to be badgered at breakfast by Asuka.

[]:             Quote Post by fadingreminder » Wed Mar 14, 2007 12:47 pm This is quickly launching from a universal Unit-03 conspiracy topic to a general "Conspiracies of Evangelion" topic... I like it. I'm not convinced that Toji's mother's soul is in Unit-03, nor am I convinced that his family is involved with/works for NERV. You've done a pretty good job of discrediting my ideas and theories as for the set-up though. I still probably will end up blaming Seele, just because I see them as puppet masters and the closest thing to a "villain" I can apply the term to in Eva. I can't prove it, just as it's not possible to disprove it. Things like this, all we can do is credit and discredit, I suppose... I overuse the elipses...

[]:             Quote Post by Fearthebait » Wed Mar 14, 2007 3:25 pm you know, SEELE could have known it was contaminated the whole time and just had NERV rush picking a candidate as to force the angel to emerge and attack.

[]:             Quote Post by C4 » Wed Mar 14, 2007 4:56 pm Fearthebait wrote: you know, SEELE could have known it was contaminated the whole time and just had NERV rush picking a candidate as to force the angel to emerge and attack.
